meaning: n.压,按,印刷,压力,拥挤,紧握,新闻vt.压,压榨,紧抱,逼迫vi.压,逼迫,拥挤,受压
examples: He gave the bell another press.
The press could scent a scandal.
"A letter was written by press officers and I authorised that action, " he said.
Blunt was a virtually innocent victim, we were told, and the only villain was the press.
